summary: Completed comprehensive testing of new portfolio features: navigation anchor links, terminal functionality, contact CTA section, and JSON-LD structured data. All major features working correctly with one minor CSS issue.

design_issues.issue: scroll-margin-top: 80px CSS rule in globals.css not being applied - sections scroll to 0px instead of 80px offset

passed_tests: All navigation section links visible on desktop (About, Work, Projects, Skills, Terminal, Contact)
Navigation links properly hidden on mobile with md:flex responsive class
Smooth scroll navigation working for all sections
Active section highlighting in amber/orange working correctly as user scrolls
Terminal section renders with macOS-style title bar (red/yellow/green buttons)
Terminal displays welcome message 'Welcome to shubhankar.sh v1.0.0'
Terminal 'help' command shows all available commands
Terminal 'about' command shows Shubhankar's profile info
Terminal 'ls projects/' command shows project listing with indian-desi-llm
Terminal 'cat skills' command shows skills JSON with backend/data/devops/ml_ai categories
Terminal 'contact' command shows email and social links
Terminal 'neofetch' command shows ASCII art and system info
Terminal 'sudo hire shubhankar' easter egg works with offer letter message
Terminal 'clear' command properly clears output
Terminal shows error message for unknown commands
Terminal command history works with arrow keys (up/down)
Contact section shows 'Get In Touch' heading and main CTA text
Contact 'Email Me' button has correct mailto:tiwarishubhankar@gmail.com href
Contact 'LinkedIn' and 'Resume' buttons present and functional
JSON-LD structured data present with Person schema, correct name and job title
All section IDs exist: about, experience, projects, skills, terminal, contact
Mobile responsiveness working - terminal functional on mobile devices
Previously working features still functional: theme toggle, recruiter mode
action_item_for_main_agent: Minor CSS issue: scroll-margin-top: 80px rule in globals.css is not being applied to sections. Sections scroll to 0px position instead of 80px offset from top. This might be a CSS specificity issue or Next.js CSS compilation issue.
